entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., the primary crop studied for seed longevity

entities.name: ICRISAT GENEBANK
entities.description: The facility where peanut germplasm accessions are conserved

entities.name: MEDIUM-TERM STORAGE
entities.description: Storage conditions characterized by 4 C and 30% RH

entities.name: SHORT-TERM STORAGE
entities.description: Storage conditions characterized by 23-25 C and 40-50% RH

entities.name: AMBIENT STORAGE
entities.description: Storage conditions characterized by 20-40 C and 30-80% RH

entities.name: SEED LONGEVITY
entities.description: The duration for which peanut seeds remain viable and capable of germination

entities.name: IN-SHELL SEEDS
entities.description: Peanut seeds conserved within their pods

entities.name: SHELLED SEEDS
entities.description: Peanut seeds conserved without their pods

entities.name: ICGS 76
entities.description: A virginia bunch peanut cultivar used in the study

entities.name: JL24
entities.description: A spanish peanut cultivar used in the study

entities.name: LOW MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: Moisture levels below 4%, specifically 3.6%, used to extend seed life

entities.name: HERMETIC SEALING
entities.description: A method of sealing seeds to maintain low moisture content

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The ability of peanut seeds to sprout, used as a measure of viability

entities.name: REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of growing out stored seeds to replenish stocks

entities.name: POD STORAGE
entities.description: The practice of conserving peanut seeds as pods

entities.name: SEED STORAGE
entities.description: The practice of conserving peanut seeds without pods

relationships.description: is compared between in-shell and shelled seeds
is compared between in-shell and shelled seeds
